title: Sampling, Inference, Regression, and Causal Limits · Model Lens: Study Design
summary: Evaluate a program change from sampled observational and experimental evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int study design increment in an inference and limitations brief. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish observational studies, experiments, sampling frames, randomization, and blocking.
Design reproducible simulations and quantify Monte Carlo sampling error.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Study Design evidence
steps.instruction: The observed values are 14, 18, 22, 18 minutes. Calculate their mean. Use the result to decide Study Design for an inference and limitations brief.
steps.why: The Study Design result for an inference and limitations brief is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Add all 4 observations and divide by 4; retain the time unit. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish observational studies, experiments, sampling frames, randomization, and blocking.
